skill.name: context-degradation
skill.description: Helps AI practitioners identify and mitigate context degradation patterns in long conversations and agent workflows.
skill.summary: Diagnose lost-in-middle, context poisoning, distraction, confusion, and clash in AI agent contexts.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static findings to be false positives caused by Markdown examples, metadata, and ordinary prose. The skill is a documentation-only guide and contains no executable scripts, runtime network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts. It is safe to publish, with normal editorial review for factual freshness.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged backtick markers in example blocks as Ruby or shell execution. The surrounding content is instructional Markdown showing token counts and context layout, not runnable command code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fence delimiters and prose examples. No command interpreter, shell invocation, or user-controlled execution path exists.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Metadata URL Flagged as Network Access
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL is source metadata in the skill frontmatter. No code in the skill performs HTTP requests or transmits data to that URL.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only in the declared source field. The package contains no executable network client or runtime fetch behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches in Prose
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm patterns across ordinary explanatory text. The skill does not implement cryptography, select algorithms, process secrets, or advise use of weak crypto.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of each cited line shows discussion of context behavior, model selection, or general guidance. There is no cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or security configuration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Wording in Conceptual Text
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged a line about multi-source retrieval and version conflicts. This is conceptual guidance about conflicting information sources, not host inspection or environment reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text contains no filesystem, process, network, or operating system enumeration. It describes context clash in AI retrieval workflows.

content.value_statement: Long AI conversations can lose accuracy when important context is buried, stale, irrelevant, or conflicting. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured way to recognize failure patterns and choose mitigations.
actual_capabilities: Explains lost-in-middle behavior and why critical information should sit near context edges.
Identifies context poisoning, distraction, confusion, and clash as distinct failure patterns.
Maps common symptoms to recovery actions such as truncation, compaction, filtering, and isolation.
Provides practical context architecture patterns for long conversations and agent workflows.
Offers example structures for placing task goals, detailed context, and key findings.
limitations: It is an educational guide and does not run diagnostics automatically.
Model benchmark claims may need review as model releases change.
It references a detailed patterns document that is not included in this package.
It does not replace task-specific evaluation data from production systems.

use_cases.title: Debug Long Agent Sessions
use_cases.description: Identify whether failures come from buried instructions, stale assumptions, irrelevant context, or conflicting sources.

use_cases.title: Design Context Loading Rules
use_cases.description: Plan what information enters the active context, what stays external, and when compaction should happen.

use_cases.title: Review Prompt Workflows
use_cases.description: Assess long prompts and multi-turn workflows for context placement, distraction, and clash risks.

prompt_templates.title: Identify the Pattern
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner diagnosis of a failed long conversation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this AI conversation failure. Identify whether it shows lost-in-middle, poisoning, distraction, confusion, or clash. Explain the evidence in simple terms.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Context Placement
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt cleanup for important information.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this prompt structure. Move critical goals, constraints, and final facts to attention-favored positions while keeping supporting details organized.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Recovery Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced remediation after degraded agent behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these symptoms and context excerpts, propose a recovery plan. Include what to remove, what to verify, what to summarize, and what to isolate.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Context Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced system design for long-running agents.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a context management strategy for this agent workflow. Define write, select, compress, and isolate rules, plus triggers for evaluation and compaction.

output_examples.input: A support agent starts using outdated policy text after a long session with many retrieved documents.
output_examples.output: The likely pattern is context clash with possible distraction. The response should separate current policy from outdated material, mark conflicts, and reload only verified sources.

output_examples.input: An agent ignores a key requirement placed in the middle of a very long prompt.
output_examples.output: The likely pattern is lost-in-middle. Move the requirement near the beginning or final checklist, then test recall at increasing context lengths.

output_examples.input: A coding agent keeps repeating an incorrect assumption created by an earlier summary.
output_examples.output: The likely pattern is context poisoning. Remove or replace the bad summary, preserve only verified facts, and restart the reasoning from clean context.

best_practices: Place critical goals, constraints, and final facts at the beginning or end of active context.
Filter retrieved material before adding it to the context window.
Use compaction and isolation before context length causes visible quality loss.
anti_patterns: Adding every available document to context without relevance checks.
Treating model summaries as verified facts after errors appear.
Mixing unrelated tasks in one long session without clear transitions.

faq.question: What problem does this skill address?
faq.answer: It helps identify why long AI conversations and agent sessions become less accurate or less relevant.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown guide with concepts, examples, and mitigation advice.

faq.question: Can it detect context degradation automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a diagnostic framework that a user or agent can apply to observed behavior.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What are the main degradation patterns?
faq.answer: The skill covers lost-in-middle, context poisoning, distraction, confusion, and clash.

faq.question: What should users verify before relying on it?
faq.answer: Users should verify current model benchmark claims and test mitigation strategies on their own workflows.
